channel.title: SecurityWizardry Vulnerability Radar (RMFconsultoria)
channel.description: Alertas do SecurityWizardry.com Radar Page - Curado por RMFconsultoria

item.title: RedHat - Patch - CVSS Score v3 - Multiple
item.description: Wednesday 17 June 2026 | Patch | CVSS Score v3 - Multiple | A critical Red Hat kernel update for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 8.4 addresses multiple vulnerabilities, including denial-of-service, memory corruption, and use-after-free issues.

item.title: Rockwell - OT - CVSS Score v3 - 8.7-8.8
item.description: Wednesday 17 June 2026 | OT | CVSS Score v3 - 8.7-8.8 | Two vulnerabilities in Rockwell Automation FLEX I/O EtherNet/IP adapters: a denial-of-service flaw and a critical authentication bypass that could enable unauthorized access and account takeover.

item.title: IBM - Patch - CVSS Score: v4 - Multiple
item.description: Wednesday 17 June 2026 | Patch | CVSS Score: v4 - Multiple | IBM Cloud Pak for Data System has critical cryptography flaws that may allow certificate bypass and memory corruption leading to unauthorized access or service disruption.

item.title: Fortra - Security - CVSS Score v3 - 9.8
item.description: Tuesday 16 June 2026 | Security | CVSS Score v3 - 9.8 | Fortra Core Privileged Access Manager controls privileged access; OS command injection in boks_autoregisterd allows remote code execution with service privileges.

item.title: Zyxel - Patch - CVSS Score v3 - 8.8
item.description: Tuesday 16 June 2026 | Patch | CVSS Score v3 - 8.8 | Zyxel GS1900-24EP network switch; stack-based buffer overflow in CGI allows unauthenticated LAN attacker to execute OS commands via HTTP request.

item.title: Wertheim - Patch - CVSS Score: v4 - 8.6
item.description: Tuesday 16 June 2026 | Patch | CVSS Score: v4 - 8.6 | Wertheim SafeController 5400 secures vault rooms; lacks cryptographic RS-485 protection, allowing replay attacks to spoof and disable alarms.

item.title: IBM - Patch - CVSS Score v3 - Multiple
item.description: Tuesday 16 June 2026 | Patch | CVSS Score v3 - Multiple | Multiple Axios flaws in IBM Planning Analytics enable data tampering, SSRF, DoS, and security bypass. More info.... The Vulnerability Definitions Explained

item.title: MISP - Security - CVSS Score v4 - 8.8
item.description: Monday 15 June 2026 | Security | CVSS Score v4 - 8.8 | MISP threat intelligence platform: Authenticated users could exploit mass assignment flaws to alter ownership, modify events, or access sensitive data.

item.title: Google - Patch - CVSS Score v4 - 9.4
item.description: Monday 15 June 2026 | Patch | CVSS Score v4 - 9.4 | Google MCP Toolbox for Databases manages database models; versions lacked origin validation, risking DNS rebinding; new flags improve security.

item.title: Nuxt - Patch - CVSS Score: v4 - 8.8
item.description: Monday 15 June 2026 | Patch | CVSS Score: v4 - 8.8 | Nuxt is a Vue.js web framework. Nuxt has route-rule middleware bypass via case-sensitivity mismatch.

item.title: Netty - Patch - CVSS Score v3 - 8.7
item.description: Monday 15 June 2026 | Patch | CVSS Score v3 - 8.7 | Netty network framework for protocol servers and clients; DnsResolveContext fails to validate CNAME origin in DNS responses. More info.... The Vulnerability Definitions Explained

item.title: CyberArk - Security - CVSS Score v3 - 9.1
item.description: Friday 12 June 2026 | Security | CVSS Score v3 - 9.1 | CyberArk Conjur Cloud manages secrets and access; versions allow remote attackers to bypass identity checks and steal access tokens.

item.title: Ubiquiti - Patch - CVSS Score v3 - 8.1-9.9
item.description: Friday 12 June 2026 | Patch | CVSS Score v3 - 8.1-9.9 | UID Enterprise Agent identity agent; low‑privilege user can run commands on host. UniFi OS; flaws allow sensitive file read, privilege escalation, and command injection.

item.title: Google - Patch - CVSS Score: v4 - 9.4
item.description: Friday 12 June 2026 | Patch | CVSS Score: v4 - 9.4 | Google Cloud Dialogflow CX manages conversational AI; a missing authorization flaw in playbook import lets users escalate privileges and control projects.

item.title: IBM - Patch - CVSS Score 9.1-10.
item.description: Friday 12 June 2026 | Patch | CVSS Score 9.1-10. | IBM i OS for enterprise systems; unqualified library call allows user-controlled code to run with administrator privileges, risking escalation. More info.... The Vulnerability Definitions Explained Definitions - Severity GUARDED This alert state represents the return towards normalisation of an alert state, indicating that there was a higher alert state due to a product vulnerability during the previous few days. INCREASED This alert state indicates that a product vulnerability has been identified within the last few days. The vulnerability is either difficult to exploit, or if exploited, results in reduced impact to the target system. This alert state indicates a more serious vulnerability which is exploitable. CRITICAL This alert state indicates a significant threat to the product, where exploits exist or where the vulnerability is potentially devastating.

item.title: Definitions - Type - Monthly - N/A
item.description: Friday 12 June 2026 | Monthly | N/A | Vendors of cyber security products should know better and given their importance they are highlighted when vulnerable, often combined with critival severity +24hrs This bottom descriptor is used with Indicates an alert state which has been present for more than 24 hours. This bottom descriptor indicates that patches are available for vulnerabilities, whether it is the initial report or a patch of a vulnerability that had been previously reported. This bottom descriptor indicates that an Exploit has been made public for a vulnerability, whether it is the initial report or an indication of an exploit for a vulnerability that had been previously reported. This bottom descriptor indicates that the vulnerable product is Operational Technology (OT) such as an Industrial Control System (ICS). OT is not to be confused with Information Technology (IT) This bottom descriptor indicates that a vulnerability has been announced without the opportunity for the vendor to patch it before the details are made known. Whilst vulnerabilities reported are remotely exploitable, there are rare occasions when we will report on a vulnerability with a locally exploitable attack vector (AV:L) Several vendors release multiple patches on or around the same day each month. The severity level will reflect the highest vulnerability © Computer Network Defence Limited 2026 Privacy Notice Terms & Conditions Send us a Message
